problem.title: Add Digits
problem.description: Given an integer num, repeatedly add all its digits until the result has only one digit, and return it.

example.explanation: 3+8=11 → 1+1=2. The process repeats until a single digit remains.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Mathematical [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: The repeated digit sum is the digital root, which follows a simple modular arithmetic pattern: any non-zero number's digital root equals its value modulo 9, except multiples of 9 whose root is 9 itself. This eliminates the need for any iteration or recursion, giving an O(1) solution. The key insight is that the digital root is preserved by the modulo-9 property of our decimal number system.
